The eight times German Champions Borussia Dortmund will travel to Asia again as part of their preparations for season 2017/2018. Two high profile friendly matches have been arranged between the 13. and the 19. July.
🇯🇵 BVB have been invited by the Japanese professional league to take part in the J.League (Japan Professional Football League)/Jリーグ International Series and they will play the Urawa Red Diamonds in the Saitama stadium on the 15. July.

🇨🇳 Three days later on the 18. July the Black Yellows will face the Italian traditional club A.C. Milan in the South Chinese Metropolis Guangzhou. This game is part of the International Champions Cup that BVB has been invited to take part in just as last year.

Carsten Cramer, the BVB Director for Sales and Marketing said: "we are happy to be guests in Asia again this year. We look back on our past visits with fond memories and we know: we will meet many real true and extremely loyal BVB fans in Japan and also in China. In conjunction with our longterm partners Evonik and PUMA Football it is our aim to excite them with the performances of our team and also with the appearances of our players." And to add to that, Hans-Joachim Watzke, the BVB CEO noted: "the fact that the J. League and the organisers of the ICC have chosen us for their events shows the high standing BVB has achieved throughout the world of sports."
